message:
fix(codegen): Handle type aliases and operator functions in call generation

- Type aliases (e.g., F = fractions.Fraction) now correctly use .init()
  for constructor calls instead of .call()
- Fraction constructors handle 0, 1, or 2 arguments correctly:
  - F() -> Fraction.init(0, 1)
  - F(n) -> Fraction.fromInt(n)
  - F(n, d) -> Fraction.init(n, d)
- Operator function parameters (op, func, cmp, fn, f) no longer get
  .init() appended - they are called directly as functions
